mnemonics.title: PUE Formula
mnemonics.content: PUE = Total Facility Energy / IT Equipment Energy - Remember: 'Put Utmost Effort' to get PUE close to 1 (Put = PUE, Utmost = Unit, Effort = Energy, close to 1 = Perfect)

mnemonics.title: Green Data Center Elements
mnemonics.content: RACE: Renewable energy, AI optimization, Cooling efficiency, Energy-aware scheduling

acronyms.usage: Measures overall data center energy efficiency (closer to 1 is better)
Measures carbon emissions per kWh of IT energy consumed
Measures water consumption for cooling in data centers
Tradable certificate proving renewable energy was generated and used
Virtualized computing resource that runs on physical hardware
Integrated system combining compute, storage, and networking
Organization that developed PUE and other sustainability metrics

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a pie chart: The smaller the non-IT slice (cooling, power loss), the closer PUE is to 1.0. Draw concentric circles with IT at center (core) and facility energy as outer rings.
Think of a apartment building (physical server) with many empty rooms (VMs). Consolidation is like moving tenants into fewer buildings to save electricity for common areas.
Traditional = renting a full hotel room 24/7 whether you stay or not. Serverless = paying only for hours you actually sleep in the room.
Think of a tree: leaves (edge devices) process some sap locally, only sending essential nutrients to the trunk (cloud), reducing travel distance.

analogies.analogy: Like a factory: IT equipment is the production line (core work), while cooling and power distribution are like HVAC and utilities - necessary overhead.
Like a multiplex theater showing multiple movies on one building, virtualization runs multiple 'computing movies' on one 'building' (server).
Like a car: 'Embodied carbon' is the emissions from manufacturing (building the data center), 'operational carbon' is the emissions from driving (running the data center).
Like doing laundry: Run high-energy appliances when solar power is available (sunny midday) or grid is cleaner (night when demand is low).
